title: phlox divaricata subsp. divaricata
intro: Phlox divaricata subsp. divaricata, also known as woodland phlox or wild blue phlox, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Phlox divaricata subsp. divaricata has been employed for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving anxiety and insomnia, and providing antioxidant effects. Herbal preparations of Phlox divaricata subsp. divaricata often involve infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used internally or applied topically to utilize its medicinal properties.
